notes: Downloads on Windows are truly fixed this time: the app now installs FFmpeg by itself (update the app to v0.22.52), so every track converts to a real mp3 with embedded artwork instead of silently piling up raw m4a/mp4 files while the counter told you stories. Download progress bars also work for the first time ever with yt-dlp — the old progress parsing never matched what yt-dlp actually prints, so bars sat at 0% and teleported to 100 — and each bar now only moves forward (no more jumping back between the audio and artwork phases). All the recent speed work still applies: up to 4 tracks at once, 4 connections per track.
